english: a good person is known by deeds
the forest gives, the forest takes
a little is better than nothing
in which room did you sleep?
he wrote well in the exam.
what is written in the letter?
why do you drive the car?
why did she pay the money?
why did he bring the bag?
i saw the film last week.
garo: Ang·a ia film ko nia mija antio.

english: why did you tell him to go?
why are they late for the meeting?
garo: Bisong maina meeting ona ru·ute reba·a?

english: how many apples are there in my hand?
how much did he pay you?
which indian state has the largest population?
garo: India ni badiya adoko mande bangbata?

english: can you give me your pen?
garo: Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?

english: at the market / to the market

garo: Never translate English SVO word order directly into Garo

english: people say it's good, is it good?
in a broad daylight are you sleeping?
no need to thank me, you're welcome

garo: The rakka (·) is a phonetic marker belonging to the root word. Suffixes are always clean strings.
Words with multiple meanings depending on context
